MCQ with papers provided. MCQ; papers provided. Closed-book solvability fails because several options labeled Correct (A, B, C, E, F) require paper-specific rankings and causal attributions (e.g., LBV order LCO/NMC > NMC > LFP; lean-vs-rich overpressure sensitivity; LFP vs NCA explosion propensity mechanism) that are not stated in the stem and are not derivable from general engineering reasoning. The stem only asserts LFP has higher explosion propensity than NMC at stoichiometry, which is insufficient to uniquely justify the marked Correct options.

Closed-book solvability fails because the stem asks to “address the mechanism governing ignition delay time in each temperature regime,” but neither the stem nor the evidence bundle provides any mechanism information (only empirical temperature-sensitivity/regime attenuation statements). Any mechanism-based explanation would require paper-specific chemistry/kinetic details not included, making the short-answer rubric not fully answerable from the admissible information set.
